original: Assassination of Conrad of Montferrat (Conrad I), King of Jerusalem, in Tyre, two days after his title to the throne confirmed by election. Killing carried out by Hashshashin.
-May 7th) Utrecht destroyed by fire
English "Good Parliament" begins meeting in London, will sit till 10th July, then the longest-sitting English parliament
English parliament demands supervision of royal spending
Battle at Cerignalo: Spanish army under Gonzalo Fernández de Córdoba defeats a French force led by Louis d'Armagnac, Duke of Nemours
Treaty of Worms: Emperor Charles names his brother Ferdinand Archduke of Netherlands-Austria

Establishment of the Pontifical and Royal University of Santo Tomas, the Catholic University of the Philippines, the oldest existing university in Asia and the largest Catholic university in the world
Virginia Governor John Harvey accused of treason and removed from office
English General-at-Sea Robert Blake (he was never titled Admiral) beats North African pirate fleet
Dutch ship Vergulde Draeck (Gilt Dragon) laden with eight chests of silver, wrecks on the western coast of Australia with the loss of 118 lives
French-Canadian explorer Louis Jolliet sets out from Quebec leading the first documented journey of the Labrador coastline (reaches Zoar area)
82 survivors of the Dutch ship Zeewijk sail into Batavia on a hand-built boat, ten months after being shipwrecked off the western coast of Australia
31 British ships sail up the Suriname River, demanding the transfer of the colony from the Dutch
Dutch parliament accepts new press laws
George B. Vashon becomes 1st African American to enter New York State Bar
Slavery abolished in French colonies
First veterinary college in the US is incorporated in Boston
Giacomo Meyerbeer's opera "L'Africaine" premieres in Paris
French troops sent into Tunisia
Cleveland's Bock Baker gives up a record 23 singles as White Sox beat Blues (Cleveland Blues!) 13-1

Using the ISO 8601 standard Year Zero definition for the Gregorian calendar preceded by the Julian calendar, the one billionth minute since the start of January 1 Year Zero occurs at 10:40 AM on this date
181 die in coal mine collapse at Eccles, West Virginia
American stuntman and parachute developer Leslie Irvin (23) makes the first premeditated free-fall parachute jump from a plane above the US Army Air Service's McCook Field near Dayton, Ohio
Azerbaijan SSR joins USSR (1st time)
WOI (Ames, Iowa) country's 1st licensed educational radio station
119 die in coal mine disaster at Benwood, West Virginia
Kurdish rebels surrender to Turkish army
Netherlands & Great Britain return to gold standard

RCA and GE install three test television sets in homes in Schenectady, New York, allowing trials of inventor E.F.W. Alexanderson's first home television receiver; a poor and unsteady 1.5-square-inch picture is received from a radio transmitter
First night of organized baseball is played in Independence, Kansas
Program for woman athletes approved for 1932 Olympics track & field
Soccer team Blue White '34 forms
Spanish government of Ricardo Samper forms
Tigers' Goose Goslin grounds into 4 straight double plays
Moscow underground railway opens (81 km long)
First animated cartoon electric sign is displayed in NYC
First commercial flight across the Pacific is operated by Pan Am
SS-Obersturmbannführer (lieutenant colonel) Rudolf Höss (not Hess, different Nazi) becomes commandant of concentration camp Auschwitz
Last British troops in Greece surrender
"WWII" continues to be titled so as a result of a Gallup Poll after US PresidentFranklin Rooseveltsuggests other names
German Luftwaffe estimated to have flown over 11 thousand sorties against Malta since 20th March
Nightly "dim-out" begins along US East Coast, as a deterance to German military threats
1st performance of Marc Blitzstein's "Freedom Morning"
German-Italian counter offensive in North Africa
US 34th Division occupies Djebel el Hara, North Tunisia
Exercise "Tiger" ends with 750 US soldiers dead in D-Day rehearsal after their convoy ships were attacked by German torpedo boats off Slapton Sands, Devon.
British commandos attack Elbe & occupy Lauenburg
US 5th army reaches Swiss border
Former Philippine First Lady Aurora Quezon, 61, assassinated while en route to dedicate a hospital in memory of her late husband; her daughter and 10 others also killed
St Louis Browns lend 2 black minor leaguers to Hankyu Braves of Japan
WW II Pacific peace treaty takes effect
WBIQ TV channel 10 in Birmingham, Alabama (PBS) begins broadcasting
Last French troops leave Vietnam
WSOC TV channel 9 in Charlotte, North Carolina (ABC) begins broadcasting
Great Britain performs atmospheric nuclear test at Christmas Island
Vanguard TV-5 launched for Earth orbit (failed)
KLOE TV channel 10 in Goodland, KS (CBS) begins broadcasting
KPLR TV channel 11 in Saint Louis, MO (IND) begins broadcasting
Lt Col Gueorgui Mossolov takes E-66A to 34,714 m altitude
17th Tony Awards: "Who's Afraid of Virginia Woolf?" (play) and "A Funny Thing Happened on the Way to the Forum" (musical) win
Japan joins the OECD
Lindsey Nelson broadcasts baseball game at Houston Astrodome from a hanging gondola
Richard Helms replaces Marshall S. Carter as deputy director of CIA
US marines invade Dominican Republic, stay until October 1966
William F. Raborn Jr. replaces John A. McCone as 7th head of CIA
Cleveland ties record with 10th straight win since Opening Day
OCAM, Common Afro-Mauritian Organization forms
British progressive rock band King Crimson with Robert Fripp, Greg Lake & Ian McDonald debuts
Dutch social democratic party/D'66/DS'70 win parliamentary election
Samuel Lee Gravely Jr. becomes 1st black admiral in US Navy

Over 6000 Mk. 82 500 pound bombs detonate over 18 hrs in a railyard in northern California. 5500 structures damaged, town of Antelope destroyed, with every building reduced to foundations. Leads to Transportation Safety Act (1974)
South-Vietnam Gen Duong Van Minh sworn in as president till April 30
American defense employee Christopher John Boyce convicted for selling secrets to the Soviet Union
The Budapest Treaty on the International Recognition of the Deposit of Microorganisms for the Purposes of Patent Procedure is signed
Cyrus Vance, Carter's US Secretary of State, resigns
Reunion Arena in Dallas opens
US Secretary of State Cyrus Vance resigns in protest over the Iran hostage rescue attempt
"We’re Fighting Back" premieres on CBS in the US, based on the formation of the Guardian Angels
Galician current Statute of Autonomy
Argentine government declares all 15-30,000 missing persons dead
NASA launches Geos-F
American engineer Ben Linder is killed in an ambush by U.S.-funded Contras in northern Nicaragua.
NBA announces expansion to Charlotte NC & Miami Fla in 1988 & Minneapolis Minn & Orlando Fla in 1989
NFL Draft: University of Miami quarterback Vinny Testaverde first pick by Tampa Bay Buccaneers
Aloha Airlines Boeing 737 roof tears off in flight; kills stewardess
Baltimore Orioles lose AL record 21 games in a row
NJ Devils set all time playoff mark for penalty minutes
Argentina, hit by rocketing inflation, runs out of money
Boston Celtics score most points in a playoff, beat NY Knicks 157-128
Last issue of Dutch communist daily De Waarheid (The Truth)
Professional Bowlers Association Tournament of Champions: Dave Ferraro beats Tony Westlake, 226–203
Space Shuttle STS 39 (Discovery 12) launched
Italian President Francesco Cossiga formally resigns
Carlo Ciampi forms Italian government with ex-communists
Zambian national football team dies in a plane crash near Libreville, Gabon that kills 30 people
Freddy Thielemans sworn in as Mayor of Brussels, Belgium
Gas explosion in South Korean metro, 103 die
Sri Lankaan BAE748 crashes at Palaly, 52 die
In Australia's worst massacre in modern history, Martin Bryant shoots and kills 35 in Port Arthur, Tasmania. Leads to a compulsory gun buy back program and major changes to gun control laws.
"Jekyll & Hyde" opens at Plymouth Theater NYC
Ringo Starr's fourth All-Starr Band debuts in concert; members include: Peter Frampton, Jack Bruce, Gary Brooker, Simon Kirke, and Mark Rivera
Millionaire Dennis Tito becomes the world's first space tourist
The Tradition Senior Men's Golf, Superstition Mountain G&CC: Jim Thorpe wins his lone career major title with birdie on first playoff hole against John Jacobs
The Patent Law Treaty goes into effect.
ICC Men's Cricket ODI World Cup, Kensington Oval, Bridgetown, Barbados: Australia 3-peat with 53 run win over Sri Lanka; Player of the Match: Adam Gilchrist (AUS) 149 (104) & 2 catches
NFL Draft: LSU quarterback JaMarcus Russell first pick by Oakland Raiders
NFL Draft: Auburn quarterback Cam Newton first pick by Carolina Panthers
Tent collapse in St Louis, Missouri, kills one and injures 110 people
3 people are killed and 14 are injured after a gas explosions causes a building to collapse in Reims, France
8 people are killed and dozens are injured after Taliban attacks on election candidates in Pakistan
NFL Draft: Cal quarterback Jared Goff first pick by Los Angeles Rams
Indian government announces electricity has now reached every Indian village
Shaquem Griffin is the first one-handed person to be drafted into the NFL; selected by Seattle Seahawks
World's largest child sacrifice, 140 remains uncovered by archaeologists near Trujillo, Peru, dating back 550 years to Chimú civilisation
American diver Victor Vescovo makes the deepest dive ever to the bottom of the Mariana trench at 10,927m (35,849ft), and finds a plastic bag
Largest demonstrations since 2014 in Hong Kong over plans to change law to send suspects to mainland China for trial
Argentina bans all commercial domestic and international flights until September 1 because of COVID-19
US confirmed cases of COVID-19 pass 1 million, while death toll of 58,365 surpasses that of US soldiers killed in Vietnam War (Johns Hopkins)
US Department of Defense releases three declassified videos of possible UFOs from 2004 and 2015
NASA's Parker Solar Probe becomes the first spacecraft to cross the Alfvén critical boundary, the outer atmosphere of the Sun
Taiwanese political TV drama "Wave Makers" debuts on Netflix - goes on to ignite a #Me Too movement in Taiwan
A worse than usual rainy season across East Africa due to El Nino  kills at least 155 people in Tanzania and 60 in Kenya with hundreds of thousands displaced
At least 37 mostly nocturnal tornadoes cause widespread devastation and four deaths in Oklahoma
Airports, hospitals, and public transit shut down due to a widespread power outage in Spain, Portugal, Andorra, and parts of France
Canadian voters reelect the Liberal Party to run the government, prompted by trade war with US and comments by US political leaders made about annexing their country
EmperorConstantius IIarrives in Rome for his first and only visit
The Palatine Chapel, commissioned byRoger IIand one of Norman Sicily's most important buildings is consecrated on Palm Sunday in Palermo (World Heritage Site 2015)
English ambassador William Norris is granted an audience with Mughal EmperorAurangzebin attempt to gain trading rights for the New East India Company (he fails)
British CaptainJames Cook, aboardHMS Endeavour, first lands in Australia at Botany Bay
Armistice of Cherasco between Sardinia andNapoleon Bonaparteat Cherasco, Piedmont gives France territories of Piedmont, Savoy and Nice
Rush-Bagot treaty signed between US PresidentJames Monroeand Great Britain, demilitarizing the US-Canada border
Billy the Kidescapes from the Lincoln County jail in Lincoln, New Mexico
1st performance ofAntonín Dvořák's orchestral work "Carnival Overture"
First night air flight byClaude Grahame-Whitetakes place in England
CubanJosé Raúl Capablancabecomes the world's third chess champion defeating German champion Emanuel Lasker in Havana
US PresidentFDRsigns Home Owners Loan Act
Adolf Hitlerclaims German-Polish non-attack treaty still in effect
Glenn Millerrecords the song "Pennsylvania 6-5000" in NYC
Soviet leaderJoseph Stalinmeets Polish-American priest Stanislaus Orlemanski in Moscow to discuss religion and the future of post-war Poland
Thor Heyerdahland the crew of the "Kon-Tiki" sail from Peru to Polynesia
MohammadMossadeghelected Prime Minister of Iran by the Parliament of Iran (Majlis)
Dwight D. Eisenhowerresigns as Supreme Commander of NATO
Cincinnati Reds outfielderFrank Robinsonhits his 1st of 586 HRs
US Vice PresidentRichard Nixonbegins goodwill tour of Latin America
Milwaukee Braves' future HOF pitcherWarren Spahnthrows his second career no hitter at 41; beats SF Giants, 1-0 at County Stadium
"My Name is Barbra",Barbra Streisand's first television special, premieres on CBS
Luciano Pavarottimakes his debut at La Scala Teatro in Milan, Italy in Franco Zeffirelli's production of "La bohème" with Mirella Freni
38th Academy Awards: "The Sound of Music",Julie Christie&Lee Marvinwin
David Diamond's 5th Symphony premieres with the New York Philharmonic conducted byLeonard Bernstein; the work was dedicated to the conductor
Muhammad Alirefuses induction into army & stripped of boxing title
TheDouglas AircraftCompany behind schedule with deliveries of the DC-8 and DC-9 and close to bankruptcy agrees to merge with the McDonnell Aircraft Corporation to form McDonnell Douglas
Charles de Gaulleresigns as president of France
Northern Ireland Prime MinisterTerence O'Neillresigns and is replaced later byJames Chichester-Clark

Ringo Starrappears on "The Smothers Brothers Show", singing Hoyt Axton's "No No Song", andJohn Lennonis the guest onTom Snyder's "Tomorrow" program, in what turns out to be his final television interview; both on NBC-TV
Andreas Baaderand members of the terrorist group Red Army Faction (Baader-Meinhof Gang) are jailed for life after a trial lasting nearly two years in Stuttgart, Germany
Billy Martinnamed NY Yankee manager for 4th time
Fernando Valenzuelasets record of 41 scoreless inn to start season
Iran protests sale of "Satanic Verses" bySalman Rushdie
Revival of Jules Styne andStephen Sondheim's musical "Gypsy" starring Tyne Daly, re-opens at Marquis Theater, NYC, after a short break and venue transfer; runs  for an additional 105 performances
NY Islanders beat Washington Capitals 4 to 1 in playoffs, Capitals' Dale Hunter attacksPierre Turgeonafter scoring, in hockey's worst cheap shot
Aldrich Ames, former CIA officer and his wife Rosario plead guilty to spying for the Soviet Union and Russia
Andre Agassirecaptures the world #1 ranking to become the oldest top-ranked male in the history of the ATP rankings at 33 years and 13 days
Craig Fergusonannounces he will leave "The Late Late Show" at the end of 2014
Gwyneth Paltrowbrand Goop and Condé Nast announce they will be launching a quarterly publication together
Danny DeVitoDay declared in Jersey for 17th November by Mayor John Moor of Asbury Park
Disney says Marvel film "Avengers: Endgame" made estimated $1.2 billion worldwide, first film to make more than 1 billion on opening
Spanish general election: no party wins a majority, Socialists under Pedro Sanchez win most with 29%
TV series "Game of Thrones: The Long Night" episode debuts with the longest battle ever screened (nearly 80 mins), surpassing "Lord of the Rings" Battle of Helm's Deep (44 mins)
PresidentVladimir Putinwarns Russia has not yet reached the peak of COVID-19 infections, with 93,000 recorded cases and 867 deaths
US PresidentJoe Bidenmakes a major speech to a joint session of Congress calling for a new era of government spending
Russian missiles strike Ukrainian city of Kyiv during a visit by UN Secretary-GeneralAntónio Guterres
Beyoncé's 10th concert tour, The Cowboy Carter Tour, commences in Inglewood, California
Maryland becomes 7th state to ratify US constitution
Fletcher Christian leads a mutiny on HMS Bounty against its captainWilliam Blighin the South Pacific

"Europe's first motoring competition" is 'won' by The Marquis de Dion on a steam-powered quadricycle built by French toymaker and engineer Georges Bouton; French newspaper Le Velocipede organized the 'test', and Bouton was the only participant
Soviet TV news program Vremya announces a nuclear accident at Chernobyl nuclear power station, 2 days after the event
Shrek the sheep from Tarras, Central Otago, New Zealand, is finally shorn live on TV after 6 years avoidance; the fleece weighed 27 kg (60 lb)
